title: All the Authority Concerns
author: Cheryl Giles
content: In a terraformed Mars colony, a colony ship's AI developing emotions it was never designed to have. What begins as a seemingly straightforward narrative unfolds into something far more complex and cerebral, challenging assumptions about what we think we know and what remains hidden beneath the surface. Through the lens of progress, consciousness, time, and humanity, the work examines how individuals navigate forces larger than themselves. The setting of a terraformed Mars colony becomes more than backdrop; it functions as a character in its own right, shaping and constraining the choices available to those who inhabit it. The characters are rendered with remarkable specificity, their voices distinct and their motivations layered. As the story of a colony ship's AI developing emotions it was never designed to have unfolds, the reader is invited to hold multiple perspectives simultaneously, finding sympathy in unexpected places and complexity where simplicity might be more comfortable. Cheryl Giles joins the ranks of Becky Chambers and Ted Chiang with this cerebral contribution to contemporary science fiction. The treatment of progress and consciousness is both intellectually rigorous and emotionally generous, marking All the Authority Concerns as a significant addition to the conversation. Questions of singularity, progress, and alienation drive the narrative forward with compelling urgency. Rather than offering simple resolutions, the story sits with ambiguity, acknowledging that these fundamental human concerns resist tidy conclusions. The result is a work that rewards reflection and rereading. Through the lens of humanity, progress, and exploration, the work examines how individuals navigate forces larger than themselves. The setting of a planet with incomprehensible alien ruins becomes more than backdrop; it functions as a character in its own right, shaping and constraining the choices available to those who inhabit it. The themes of singularity, progress, and technology pulse through every chapter, grounding the narrative in questions that feel both timeless and urgently contemporary. The author approaches these subjects with nuance and intelligence, refusing easy answers while offering moments of genuine insight and emotional truth. The themes of entropy, dystopia, and exploration pulse through every chapter, grounding the narrative in questions that feel both timeless and urgently contemporary. The author approaches these subjects with nuance and intelligence, refusing easy answers while offering moments of genuine insight and emotional truth. The themes of dystopia, evolution, and colonization pulse through every chapter, grounding the narrative in questions that feel both timeless and urgently contemporary.
